model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Miami (FL) has the stronger overall efficiency profile in both KenPom and Torvik, with a better defensive baseline than Missouri and a comparable offense, which gives the Hurricanes a slightly higher floor in a close 7/10-type game. Missouri’s upset path is credible if it turns this into a shot-making contest and wins the three-point/tempo variance, but its weaker defensive numbers make it harder to string together stops if Miami plays to its baseline. With no clear, matchup-flipping availability news surfaced in the quick scan, I’m siding with the team that rates better on both ends.

reasoningStep.title: Two-way efficiency edge in a close matchup
reasoningStep.summary: Miami (FL) rates higher in KenPom and Torvik and has the better defensive profile, making it the safer pick unless Missouri wins a high-variance shooting game.
evidence: KenPom: Miami (FL) #30 (AdjEM 20.67; AdjO 121.4 #34; AdjD 100.7) vs Missouri #52 (AdjEM 15.36; AdjO 119.5 #50; AdjD 104.1).
Torvik: Miami (FL) #35 (AdjOE 121.06 #35; AdjDE 101.46 #45) vs Missouri #46 (AdjOE 119.85 #42; AdjDE 102.99 #68).
Quick news/injury scan did not surface a specific, verified availability issue for either team that would override the baseline efficiency edge.
Underdog path considered: Missouri can win if it controls pace and wins three-point variance, but Miami’s defensive edge provides more stability.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
